title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
summary: Small repeated actions shape identity more effectively than dramatic one-off effort.
lessons: Make good habits obvious and easy.
Reduce friction for actions you want to repeat.
Focus on identity, not just outcomes.
reflection: What is one tiny behaviour you can repeat daily this week?

title: The Happiness Advantage
author: Shawn Achor
summary: Happiness fuels better performance, stronger resilience, and more creative thinking.
lessons: Positive emotion can improve work quality and persistence.
Train your mind to notice progress and gratitude.
Small mindset habits compound over time.
Your environment shapes how optimistic and focused you feel.
reflection: What is one thing that already went well today?

title: Mindset
author: Carol S. Dweck
summary: A growth mindset helps people treat ability as improvable through effort, strategy, and feedback.
lessons: Do not confuse current performance with fixed potential.
Feedback is useful information, not a verdict.
Effort matters most when tied to adjustment and learning.
Leaders shape culture through the mindset they reward.
reflection: Where can you replace self-judgment with experimentation?
